root.name: baseten
root.description:
Command-line interface for managing Baseten resources.

Authentication is via 'baseten auth login' or the BASETEN_API_KEY environment variable.

The CLI never prompts interactively when stdin is not a terminal; commands that need input must be supplied via flags or stdin redirection or they fail fast.

children.name: api
children.description:
Make raw HTTP requests to Baseten management or inference APIs.

The HTTP method defaults to GET, or POST when --field, --raw-field, or --input is provided. JSON responses are pretty-printed by default; non-JSON responses are streamed raw. Use --jq to filter JSON responses.

children.name: management
children.description:
Make raw HTTP requests to the Baseten management API (api.baseten.co).

Paths are relative to /v1/, so 'baseten api management models' requests /v1/models.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: method
flags.description: HTTP method, defaults to GET or POST if fields are provided

flags.name: field
flags.description: Add a string field (key=value), parsed as JSON value

flags.name: raw-field
flags.description: Add a raw string field (key=value)

flags.name: header
flags.description: Add a request header (key:value)

flags.name: input
flags.description: Read request body from file (use - for stdin)

examples.description: GET a management resource.
POST a management resource with fields.

children.text_description: The HTTP response body, passed through verbatim. JSON responses are pretty-printed; non-JSON responses are streamed raw to stdout.
children.json_description: Shape depends on the requested endpoint. See the management API OpenAPI spec at https://api.baseten.co/v1/spec.

children.name: inference
children.description:
Make raw HTTP requests to a Baseten inference endpoint.

Requires either --model-id or --chain-id to identify the target. Use --environment to target a specific environment (e.g. production).

flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output

flags.name: remote-url

flags.name: method
flags.description: HTTP method, defaults to GET or POST if fields are provided

flags.name: field
flags.description: Add a string field (key=value), parsed as JSON value

flags.name: raw-field
flags.description: Add a raw string field (key=value)

flags.name: header
flags.description: Add a request header (key:value)

flags.name: input
flags.description: Read request body from file (use - for stdin)

flags.name: model-id
flags.description: Model ID to target

flags.name: chain-id
flags.description: Chain ID to target

flags.name: environment
flags.description: Environment name (e.g. production)

examples.description: POST a predict body to a model.

children.text_description: The inference endpoint's response body, passed through verbatim. JSON responses are pretty-printed; non-JSON responses are streamed raw.
children.json_description: Shape depends on the model and endpoint. See the inference API OpenAPI spec at https://api.baseten.co/inference-spec.

children.name: auth
children.description:
Log in, log out, and manage Baseten credentials.

Credentials are stored in the system keyring when available, with a plaintext fallback in the config directory.

children.name: login
children.description:
Log in to Baseten via browser (OAuth device flow) or API key.

By default, opens a browser for interactive login. Use --web to skip prompts (suitable for non-TTY environments). Use --with-api-key to provide an API key (reads from stdin, or prompts interactively if TTY).

flags.name: web
flags.description: Use browser login without interactive prompts

flags.name: with-api-key
flags.description: Read API key from stdin

flags.name: label
flags.description: Label for the API key credential

flags.name: insecure-storage
flags.description: Store credentials in plain text instead of system keyring

examples.description: Browser-based login (OAuth device flow).
Provide an API key on stdin.

jq_example.description: Print just the logged-in user's email.

children.text_description: Prints "Logged in as <email> (<workspace>)" to stdout on success.

children.name: logout
children.description: Remove stored credentials for the active user. For OAuth credentials, also revokes the session.

children.name: model
children.description:
Create, list, and push Baseten models.

Authentication is via 'baseten auth login' or the BASETEN_API_KEY environment variable.

children.name: push
children.summary: Push a model directory as a new model or deployment
children.description:
Build a model archive, upload it to Baseten, and create either a new model or a new deployment of an existing model.

The current directory is used by default; pass --dir to push a model directory at another path.

The model is identified by the `model_name` field in config.yaml. Use --override-name to override that for this push only.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: dir
flags.description: Model directory to push. Defaults to the current directory.

flags.name: team
flags.description: Team the model belongs to. Only valid for new models.

flags.name: dry-run
flags.description: Validate the push and request upload credentials without uploading or creating anything.

flags.name: promote
flags.description: Promote the new deployment to the production environment.

flags.name: environment
flags.description: Stable environment to push to. Mutually exclusive with --promote.

flags.name: deployment-name
flags.description: Human-readable name for the new deployment.

flags.name: no-build-cache
flags.description: Force a full rebuild without using cached layers.

flags.name: labels
flags.description: User-provided labels for the deployment as a JSON object, e.g. '{"team":"ml","priority":1}'.

flags.name: tail
flags.description: Stream build and runtime logs to stderr after pushing. Logs are always text-formatted; use 'baseten model deployment logs --tail' for structured log streaming.

flags.name: wait
flags.description: Block until the deployment is active. Exits non-zero on a terminal-failure status.

flags.name: watch
flags.description: Watch the model directory and push on change. (not yet implemented)

flags.name: watch-hot-reload
flags.description: Hot-reload the running container on watched changes. (not yet implemented)

flags.name: watch-keepalive
flags.description: Keep the watcher alive after the deployment exits. (not yet implemented)

flags.name: deploy-timeout
flags.description: Deployment timeout as a Go duration (e.g. 30m, 1h); allowed range 10m to 24h.

flags.name: override-name
flags.description: Override the model_name from config.yaml for this push only. The on-disk config.yaml is not modified.

flags.name: override-env-instance-type
flags.description: Use this deployment's instance type instead of preserving the target environment's. Only meaningful when an environment is targeted.

flags.name: disable-archive-download
flags.description: Disable archive download for the new model. Only valid for new models.

examples.description: Push the current directory as a new deployment.
Push and stream build/runtime logs until the deployment is active.

jq_example.description: Print the new deployment's predict URL.

children.text_description: Narrative summary on stdout: success banner, deployment status, log/predict URLs and example next-step commands. Under --output json the narrative is redirected to stderr so stdout stays a clean JSON document.
children.json_description: Under --dry-run no upload or deployment happens; the push is validated, upload credentials are requested, and stdout is the empty JSON object `{}`. Otherwise stdout is the full model+deployment result.

Fetch a model by name.

children.name: predict
children.summary: Run a prediction against a model
children.description:
POST a JSON request to a model and write the response to stdout.

Targets the production environment by default. Use --environment, --deployment-id, or --regional to target something else.

Streaming responses (Transfer-Encoding: chunked) are passed through as they arrive. For machine-readable streaming JSON from OpenAI-compatible models, use --output jsonl.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: model-id
flags.description: ID of the model.

flags.name: model-name
flags.description: Name of the model. Use --team to disambiguate when the same name exists in multiple teams.

flags.name: team
flags.description: Team name or ID. Only valid with --model-name.

flags.name: environment
flags.description: Environment to target (e.g. production, development). Defaults to production. Mutually exclusive with --deployment-id and --regional.

flags.name: deployment-id
flags.description: Specific deployment to target. Mutually exclusive with --environment and --regional.

flags.name: regional
flags.description: Regional environment name; routes via the regional hostname. Mutually exclusive with --environment and --deployment-id.

flags.name: data
flags.description: Inline JSON request body.

flags.name: file
flags.description: Path to a JSON file containing the request body. Use '-' for stdin.

flags.name: websocket
flags.description: Use the WebSocket predict endpoint. Sends the body as one frame, reads one frame back, then closes. Not for multi-message or back-and-forth sessions.

examples.description: Send an inline JSON body.
Send a request body from a file.

jq_example.description: Extract a field when the model returns JSON.

children.text_description: The model's response body, passed through verbatim. May be JSON, plain text, or binary, and may stream when the model uses chunked transfer encoding or SSE.
children.json_description: Under --output json, binary frames are base64-encoded under a 'body' key. Under --output jsonl, each SSE or binary chunk is emitted as its own record, one per line.

children.name: delete
children.description:
Delete a Baseten model and all of its deployments.

Prompts for the model name to confirm the deletion. Pass --yes to skip the prompt. When stdin is not a terminal, --yes is required.

flags.name: model-name
flags.description: Name of the model. Use --team to disambiguate when the same name exists in multiple teams.

flags.name: team
flags.description: Team name or ID. Only valid with --model-name.

flags.name: yes
flags.description: Skip the interactive confirmation prompt. Required when stdin is not a terminal.

examples.description: Delete by ID without confirmation.
Delete by name with interactive confirmation.

children.text_description: On success, prints "Deleted model <name> (<id>)" to stderr; no stdout output.

children.name: download
children.summary: Download the Truss source for a deployment
children.description:
Download the Truss source for a model deployment as an uncompressed tar.

Exactly one of --out-file or --out-dir is required. --out-file writes the raw tar bytes; --out-dir extracts the tar into the directory. Use --overwrite to replace an existing file or write into a non-empty directory.

flags.name: deployment-id
flags.description: ID of the deployment.

flags.name: out-file
flags.description: Save the Truss as an uncompressed tar file at this path.

flags.name: out-dir
flags.description: Extract the Truss tar into this directory.

flags.name: overwrite
flags.description: Allow overwriting an existing file or non-empty directory.

examples.description: Save the Truss as a tar file.
Extract the Truss into a directory.

children.text_description: Writes the Truss to disk; prints progress and the final destination path to stderr; no stdout output.
children.json_description: On success, stdout is a JSON object with either out_file or out_dir set to the path written.

children.name: promote
children.summary: Promote a deployment to an environment
children.description:
Promote a model deployment to an environment.

Defaults to the production environment. Cleanup of the previous deployment is controlled by the target environment's promotion cleanup strategy.

Prompts for yes/no confirmation. Pass --yes to skip the prompt. When stdin is not a terminal, --yes is required.

Deployments associated with an environment (e.g. production, development) and the only deployment of a model cannot be deleted server-side.

Prompts for yes/no confirmation. Pass --yes to skip the prompt. When stdin is not a terminal, --yes is required.

children.name: logs
children.summary: Stream or tail logs for a deployment
children.description:
Fetch logs for a model deployment.

By default returns logs from the server's default recent window. Use --start/--end or --since to scope the window (max 7 days). Use --tail to stream live logs until the deployment leaves a runnable state or you interrupt with Ctrl-C.

For machine-readable streaming, prefer --output jsonl over --output json.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: model-id
flags.description: ID of the model.

flags.name: model-name
flags.description: Name of the model. Use --team to disambiguate when the same name exists in multiple teams.

flags.name: team
flags.description: Team name or ID. Only valid with --model-name.

flags.name: deployment-id
flags.description: ID of the deployment.

flags.name: tail
flags.description: Stream new logs as they arrive until the deployment leaves a runnable state or you interrupt with Ctrl-C. Cannot be combined with --start, --end, or --since. For machine-readable streaming, prefer --output jsonl over --output json.

flags.name: start
flags.description: Start of the log time range. Accepts ISO 8601 (e.g. '2026-05-14', '2026-05-14T12:00:00', '2026-05-14T12:00:00Z'). Values without a timezone designator are interpreted in the local timezone. If omitted but --end is given, defaults to 7 days before --end. Window must be at most 7 days.

flags.name: end
flags.description: End of the log time range. Accepts ISO 8601; values without a timezone designator are interpreted in the local timezone. If omitted but --start is given, defaults to now. Window must be at most 7 days.

flags.name: since
flags.description: Shortcut for fetching logs from a relative time ago until now. Accepts a Go duration (e.g. '30m', '1h30m') or '<N>d' (e.g. '3d'). Maximum '7d'. Mutually exclusive with --start and --end.

examples.description: Print logs for a deployment over the last hour.
Tail live logs until the deployment leaves a runnable state.

jq_example.description: Stream just the log messages as a JSONL stream.

children.text_description: One line per log record: "[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS]: (replica) message".

children.name: terminate
children.description:
Terminate a single replica of a model deployment.

Prompts for yes/no confirmation. Pass --yes to skip the prompt. When stdin is not a terminal, --yes is required.

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: model-id
flags.description: ID of the model.

flags.name: model-name
flags.description: Name of the model. Use --team to disambiguate when the same name exists in multiple teams.

children.name: org
children.description: Manage API keys, secrets, and billing for the active organization.

children.name: list
children.description: List API keys (metadata only; key values are never returned).

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

examples.description: List all API keys in the org.

jq_example.description: Print just the prefixes of personal keys.

children.text_description: Table with columns: NAME, KEY (prefix + ****), TYPE, TEAM. When no keys exist, prints "No API keys found." to stderr.

children.name: create
children.description: Create a new API key. The key value is printed to stdout exactly once and cannot be retrieved later; capture or pipe it on creation. --model-id may be repeated to scope the key to specific models and is only valid with --type workspace-export-metrics or --type workspace-invoke.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: type
flags.description: API key category.

flags.name: name
flags.description: Optional human-readable name for the key.

flags.name: model-id
flags.description: Restrict the key to a specific model. May be repeated. Only valid with --type workspace-export-metrics or workspace-invoke.

flags.name: team
flags.description: Team name or ID to create the key in. Defaults to the organization's default team.

examples.description: Create a personal API key.
Create a workspace-invoke key scoped to specific models.

jq_example.description: Print just the raw key value.

children.text_description: Prints the raw API key value on stdout (one line). Also prints "Save this key now. It will not be shown again." to stderr.

children.name: delete
children.description: Delete an API key. Exactly one of --name or --prefix is required: --name matches the human-readable name, --prefix matches the leading characters shown in `org api-key list`.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: name
flags.description: Human-readable name of the API key to delete.

flags.name: prefix
flags.description: Prefix of the API key to delete (as shown in list).

examples.description: Delete an API key by name.
Delete by visible prefix.

jq_example.description: Print just the deleted key's prefix.

children.text_description: Prints "Deleted API key <prefix>" to stderr on success; no stdout output.

children.name: usage
children.description: Show a billing usage summary for the organization. Pass --since (relative duration, e.g. 7d, 24h) for a sliding window ending now, or --start and --end together for an explicit ISO 8601 range. The two modes are mutually exclusive. The range cannot exceed 31 days. Defaults to --since 7d.

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: since
flags.description: Relative window ending now, as a Go duration (e.g. 7d, 24h). Mutually exclusive with --start/--end.

flags.name: start
flags.description: Start of the window (ISO 8601). Requires --end. Mutually exclusive with --since.

flags.name: end
flags.description: End of the window (ISO 8601). Requires --start. Mutually exclusive with --since.

examples.description: Show usage over the last 7 days (default).
Show usage over an explicit ISO 8601 range.

children.text_description: Not yet implemented. The output shape is TBD.

children.description: List secrets (metadata only; values are never returned).

flags.name: verbose
flags.description: Enable verbose logging

flags.name: output
flags.description: Output format

flags.name: jq
flags.description: Filter JSON output with a jq expression; implies --output json (or jsonl for streamed commands)

flags.name: remote-url

flags.name: team
flags.description: Filter to a specific team by name or ID. Defaults to all teams the caller belongs to.

examples.description: List secrets across all accessible teams.
List secrets in a specific team.

children.text_description: Table with columns: NAME, TEAM, CREATED. When no secrets exist, prints "No secrets found." to stderr.

children.name: set
children.description: Create or update a secret. The value is read from stdin (or prompted interactively on a TTY). --value is supported but discouraged: it leaks the secret into shell history and `ps` output. Pass --team to target a specific team; without it the organization's default team is used.

flags.name: verbose

flags.name: remote-url
flags.description: Baseten remote URL, overrides BASETEN_REMOTE_URL (default https://app.baseten.co)

flags.name: name
flags.description: Name of the secret.

flags.name: value
flags.description: Secret value. Discouraged: leaks into shell history and process list. Prefer stdin or prompt.

flags.name: team
flags.description: Team name or ID the secret belongs to. Defaults to the organization's default team.

examples.description: Set a secret by piping its value via stdin.
Set a secret scoped to a specific team.

children.text_description: Prints "Set secret <name>" to stderr on success; no stdout output.

children.name: truss
children.description: Delegates to the truss CLI if found on PATH, otherwise shows install instructions.

examples.description: Show truss help by passing --help to truss.

children.text_description: Whatever the truss CLI writes to stdout/stderr, passed through verbatim. --output and --jq are not honored: all arguments after `truss` are forwarded to the underlying truss binary. The exit code is propagated from truss.
